items.title: Dalal Street Week Ahead: Avoid aggressive long positions; focus on capital preservation
items.summary: Indian markets experienced a significant downturn this week, with the Nifty losing over 1.28% and breaching key support levels. Volatility surged as the India VIX climbed, indicating heightened investor caution. While Indian equities showed resilience against global peers, the near-term trend appears weak, suggesting a cautious approach and focus on capital preservation for the upcoming trading sessions.

items.title: FIIs sell Indian equities worth Rs 1.14 lakh crore in March; 2026 outflow balloons to Rs 1.27 lakh crore
items.summary: Foreign investors sold Indian shares worth over Rs 1.27 lakh crore in March. This marks the worst month for such outflows. Global tensions and a weakening rupee fueled this trend. Investors are also pulling out from other Asian markets. The situation may improve if West Asian hostilities cease and crude prices fall. Indian markets saw significant drops on Friday.

items.title: F&O Talk | Sudeep Shah on why cash market trade better versus derivatives, for now. Strategy on HEG, IDBI, 4 more stocks
items.summary: Indian stock markets faced a sharp decline in March. Geopolitical tensions and rising energy prices impacted investor sentiment. Financials, auto, and consumer stocks saw significant drops. Analysts suggest caution and disciplined risk management. The Nifty and Bank Nifty experienced substantial losses. Investors are advised to monitor key support and resistance levels closely.

items.title: Goldman Sachs’ India bets slide in FY26; 5 stocks crash over 50% but one turns multibagger
items.summary: However, despite the overall slump, six stocks bucked the trend, delivering gains in the range of 20–105% over the same period. We also highlighted three newly added stocks in the December 2025 quarter, including Inox, WeWork Management, and Midwest. (Data Source: ACE Equity, Trendlyne)

items.summary: The “Quad God” is on gold medal-watch for the second time in 2026. U.S. figure skater Ilia Malinin goes for his third world title on Saturday in Prague at the 2026 ISU World Figure Skating Championships.

items.title: Elon Musk’s companies, once welcomed in Baltimore with open arms, are now getting stiff-armed—or sued
items.summary: The demise of a Boring Co tunnel to the Ravens football stadium, within hours of the city's lawsuit against xAI, is a striking illustration of how Musk’s transformation into a political lightning rod may be taking a toll on his various ventures.
